questions.text: Pyridine is an example of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Homocyclic compounds are ring structures made up of only carbon in the ring and hydrogen atoms. Examples of homocyclic aromatic compounds are Benzene, Toluene, Phenol, etc.
Heterocyclic compounds are composed of rings made of atoms of different elements. The different elements can be nitrogen, oxygen and Sulphur.

A cyclic organic compound containing all carbon atoms in ring formation is designated as a carbocyclic compound, while the cyclic compounds consisting of at least one hetero (i.e., noncarbon) atom in the ring are known as heterocyclic compounds.

An aliphatic compound or aliphatic hydrocarbon is an organic compound containing hydrogen and carbon atoms that are usually linked together in chains via single, double or triple bonds. Sometimes the chains are also in branched trains or in the form of non-aromatic structures.

questions.text: Which suffix is used for carboxylic acid:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: -al is used for aldehydes. Therefore, this option is not correct.
-oic is used for carboxylic acids. Therefore, this option is correct.
-ol is used for alcohols. Therefore, this option is correct.
-ene is used for alkenes. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: The IUPAC name for carboxylic acid is used as alkanoic acid. The suffix is -oic.

questions.text: Which of the following hydrocarbons is least reactive?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes are less reactive than alkenes but more reactive than alkanes and benzene.This is due to alkynes have less pi bond length and less exposed than alkenes pi bonds which are more exposed.
Alkanes are the simplest and least reactive hydrocarbon species containing only carbons and hydrogens.They have only sigma bond which is stronger than pi bond.
Benzene is less reactive than alkynes and alkenes but more reactive than alkanes, having conjugate pi bonds.
Alkenes are the most reactive among all because of weak and more exposed pi bonds.

questions.explanation: Alkanes are the simplest and least reactive hydrocarbon species containing only carbons and hydrogens.They have only sigma bond which is stronger than pi bond.

questions.text: _ organic compound/s can be classified as acyclic.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Isobutane (C4H10) is an acyclic alkane with a branched chain structure. It consists of four carbon atoms bonded in a linear arrangement, without any cyclic structure.
Isobutylene (C4H8) is an acyclic alkene. It contains four carbon atoms arranged in a linear chain, with a double bond between two of the carbon atoms.
Isopentyne (C5H6) is also an acyclic compound. It contains five carbon atoms arranged in a linear chain, with a triple bond between two of the carbon atoms.
Isobutane, isopentane and isobutylene are open chain acyclic compounds.

questions.explanation: Isobutane, isopentane and isobutylene are open chain acyclic compounds.

questions.text: Which of the following is the least stable carbocation?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It has three R groups(tertiary carbocation), so it is most stable.
It has no R groups(methyl) so it is least stable.
It has two R groups(secondary), so it is stable.
This one has one alkyl group, so it is stable to some extent.

questions.explanation: Alkyl groups are electron donating groups. They increase the stability of carbocation. Greater the number of alkyl groups more stable the carbocation will be. The order is Tertiary > Secondary > Primary

questions.text: Which of the following is a functional group of acid anhydride?

An anhydride is a functional group that consists of two acyl groups (R-CO-) linked by an oxygen atom. The general structure of anhydride is RCOOCOR', where R and R' represent alkyl or aryl groups.
This is the functional group of carbonyl.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct because an anhydride is a functional group that consists of two acyl groups (R-CO-) linked by an oxygen atom. The general structure of anhydride is RCOOCOR', where R and R' represent alkyl or aryl groups.

questions.text: Which among these is not associated with aliphatic compounds?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Hückel's rule" or "4n + 2 rule." This rule is typically applied to aromatic hydrocarbons, which are a specific type of cyclic, conjugated hydrocarbons. In aromatic compounds, the π-electron systems are delocalized, providing extra stability to the molecule. The most well-known example of an aromatic hydrocarbon is benzene (C6H6), which has 6 π-electrons (n = 1), following the 4n + 2 rule: 4 * 1 + 2 = 6. For aliphatic hydrocarbons (non-aromatic hydrocarbons), such as alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes, the 4n + 2 rule does not apply because these compounds do not have a cyclic and fully conjugated π-electron system. Therefore, the rule is not relevant to aliphatic hydrocarbons.
Aliphatic compounds may be straight chain.
Aliphatic compounds maybe branched chain.

options.text: Has appropriate number of H-atoms and functional groups
options.explanation: This option is correct.

questions.explanation: Aliphatic hydrocarbons can either be straught chain or branched chain. They have appropriated number of hydrogen atoms and functional groups. However, Hückel's rule" or "4n + 2 rule." This rule is typically applied to aromatic hydrocarbons, which are a specific type of cyclic, conjugated hydrocarbons. In aromatic compounds, the π-electron systems are delocalized, providing extra stability to the molecule. The most well-known example of an aromatic hydrocarbon is benzene (C6H6), which has 6 π-electrons (n = 1), following the 4n + 2 rule: 4 * 1 + 2 = 6. For aliphatic hydrocarbons (non-aromatic hydrocarbons), such as alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes, the 4n + 2 rule does not apply because these compounds do not have a cyclic and fully conjugated π-electron system. Therefore, the rule is not relevant to aliphatic hydrocarbons.

questions.text: Vital Force Theory was proposed by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Fredrick Wohler-father of organic chemitry- rejected Vital Force Theory by synthesizing urea.

Berzelius was a Swedish scientist who in 1815 proposed that organic compounds could only be produced by some special force which must be existing in a living organism and could not be prepared in a laboratory. This force was called Vital Force and this theory came to be known as Vital Force Theory.
Michael Faraday-FRS was an English scientist-who contributed to the study of electromagnetism and electrochemistry.

In 1913, Niels Bohr proposed a theory for the hydrogen atom, based on quantum theory that some physical quantities only take discrete values. Electrons move around a nucleus, but only in prescribed orbits, and If electrons jump to a lower-energy orbit, the difference is sent out as radiation.

questions.explanation: Berzelius was a Swedish scientist who in 1815 proposed that organic compounds could only be produced by some special force which must be existing in a living organism and could not be prepared in a laboratory. This force was called Vital Force and this theory came to be known as Vital Force Theory.

questions.text: Which of the following types of cracking is used to prepare gasoline oil?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Catalytic cracking produces gasoline of higher octane number and, therefore, this method is used for obtaining better quality gasoline.

Besides increasing the yield of gasoline, cracking has also produced large amounts of useful by-products, such as ethene, propene, butene and benzene. So this is not best method to prepare gasoline. By products decrease the efficiency of reaction.
Thermal cracking is particularly useful in the production of unsaturated hydrocarbons such as ethene and propene.
Thermal cracking does not gives gasoline while between catalytic and steam cracking, catalytic cracking is an efficient way of getting gasoline as main product.

questions.explanation: Catalytic cracking produces gasoline of higher octane number and, therefore, this method is used for obtaining better quality gasoline.

questions.text: Cis-trans isomers are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diastereomers are the stereoisomers that are non identical,do not have mirror images,and hence are non-superimposable on each other.
Enantiomers are the two compounds with the exact same connectivity,that are mirror images of each other but that are not identical to each other are called Enantiomers.

Cis and trans isomers are types of geometric isomers,where the functional group is placed differently with regards to the double bond.In general,a cis isomer has molecules on one side of the double bond.A trans isomer has molecules on the other side of the double bond.
Constitutional isomers have the same molecular formula but they have different connectivities.n-Butane and iso butane are examples of constitutional isomers.

questions.explanation: Cis and trans isomers are types of geometric isomers,where the functional group is placed differently with regards to the double bond.In general,a cis isomer has molecules on one side of the double bond.A trans isomer has molecules on the other side of the double bond.

questions.text: The type of isomerism arises due to shifting of proton from one atom to other in the same molecule is called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metamerism is the type of isomerism arises due to the unequal distribution of carbon atoms on either side of the functional group.Such Compounds belong to the same homologous series.Example :diethyl ether and methyl n-propyl ether .
Tautomerism is the type of isomerism arises due to shifting of proton from one atom to other in the same molecule.Example:Acetaldeyde and Ethanol

Position isomerism is the type of isomerism is due to the difference in the position of the same functional group on the carbon chain.The arrangement of carbon atoms remain same Example:Chloropropane can have two position isomers given below .
Structural isomerism is the type of isomerism is due the difference in the arrangement of atoms within the molecule.

questions.explanation: Tautomerism is the type of isomerism arises due to shifting of proton from one atom to other in the same molecule. Example:Acetaldeyde and Ethanol.
